name: prints_latest
description: Get most recent trade (bond print) per CUSIP. When: "latest", "last", "most recent", or "current" print. Params: cusips (1-25 CUSIPs). Returns: JSON mapping CUSIP -> print object (or null).

name: prints_search
description: Search individual FINRA TRACE bond trades (prints) by CUSIP or market-wide filters. When: user wants to see individual trades — specific bond history, block trades, market-wide large prints, portfolio trades, trades in a sector. Also use when: "show me trades", "what traded", "find prints", "recent trades". Requires at least one primary filter: cusip, sector, credit_grade, min_quantity, max_quantity, or portfolio_trade_only=True. Date range alone is NOT sufficient. DEFAULT BEHAVIOUR: when neither start_date nor end_date is provided, results are scoped to last 90 days. Pass a start_date or end_date to search beyond 90 days. NOT for: rankings or most-active bonds (use securities_search). NOT for: trends or time-series (use prints_group_by_period). NOT for: VWAP or volume-weighted analytics (use analytics_vwap). DATA: covers HISTORICAL (2023-2024) + EOD (daily) + REALTIME (today's intraday). For the single most recent intraday print use prints_latest instead.

min_quantity.description: minimum par value in USD (e.g. 1000000 = $1MM). Exclusive bound.

max_quantity.description: maximum par value in USD. Exclusive bound.

start_date.description: Inclusive ISO date or timestamp in US EASTERN market time (America/New_York) — see TIMEZONE below. Defaults to 90 days ago when omitted.

end_date.description: Inclusive ISO date or timestamp in US EASTERN market time.

portfolio_trade_only.description: True to filter to basket/portfolio trades only.

distinct_cusips.description: True to dedupe to ONE print per CUSIP (its largest by quantity) — e.g. "the biggest trade in each bond". ``total`` then counts distinct CUSIPs. Still NOT a ranking tool (use securities_search).

name: prints_group_by_period
description: Aggregate trading statistics by CUSIP and time period — DEFAULT for trends. When: trends, patterns, time-series, trading history, or activity over time. Also the DEFAULT for a G-spread-over-time series ("plot the G-spread", "daily G-spread chart") — pass group_by='day', include_g_spread=true. NOT for: individual trade lookup (use prints_search) or latest single print (use prints_latest). Params: - cusips (1-20). - group_by (month|week|day|quarter|year, default month). - include_g_spread (default False): on day/week/month mode, adds a per-period ``g_spread_bps`` = period avg yield − matched-tenor Treasury yield (FRED, as-of the period) via the same calculate_gspread path as securities_search / issuer_bond_snapshot. Day mode carries a higher period cap for multi-month daily series. Returns: CUSIP -> period aggregates (newest first; up to MAX_OUTPUT_DAY_PERIODS for day mode, else MAX_OUTPUT_PERIODS_PER_CUSIP) with explicit units: - sum_par: Principal amount traded (par units) - sum_notional_usd: Approximate USD notional (sum_par * price/100) plus counts, prices, yields, and (when requested) g_spread_bps. ``meta.history_floor`` marks the oldest date the daily-agg store covers.

name: get_security_stats
description: Get trading statistics (High, Low, VWAP) for a security over a period. When: "52-week high", "YTD volume", "all-time low", or general stats. Params: cusip (single CUSIP), period (3y|1y|6m|ytd|all, default 3y).

name: analytics_vwap
description: VWAP + volume-weighted yield + high/low/median stats for a security, issuer, or sector. When: VWAP / volume-weighted pricing, sector/issuer VWAP comparison, AND per-security trading stats — "52-week high", "YTD low", "all-time high", high/low/volume over a period. For a single security's stats, call with cusips=[one] and the period as window (e.g. '1y', 'ytd'). NOT for: time-series trends (use prints_group_by_period) or single latest print (use prints_latest).

cusips.description: List of CUSIPs (1-25). Required when group_by='cusip'.

issuer.description: Issuer name filter (partial match). Use with group_by='issuer'.

sector.description: GICS sector filter. Use with group_by='sector'.

name: securities_search
description: Screen the bond universe using the latest TRACE snapshot. Supports issuer/CUSIP, grade/sector, coupon/yield/price/duration/maturity, issue-date, liquidity and CP+ filters plus server-side rankings. detail='compact' is the bounded discovery/ranking projection; detail='full' adds spread, benchmark and authorised CP+ fields. Activity quantity/notional metrics estimate finalized capped prints with FINRA prior-month IG/HY and standard/144A category averages; individual prints remain 5MM+/1MM+. maturity_ladder_years builds one server-ranked row per requested year. For exactly one resolved bond, include_recent_prints/include_cpp_history attach verified related data in the same call. include_period_history='day'|'week'|'month' batches history for at most five returned tranches. NOT for arbitrary historical as-of price snapshots.

maturity_years.description: Relative years-to-maturity range; preserve both requested endpoints.

maturity_ladder_years.description: Comma-separated years or ascending year range for one-call ladder selection.

detail.description: compact = small first-pass discovery/ranking projection; full = complete enriched record. Reuse identical filters to expand.

include_recent_prints.description: For exactly one resolved bond, attach recent TRACE prints under related_data.

include_cpp_history.description: For exactly one resolved bond, attach authorised CP+ history under related_data.

include_period_history.description: day, week, or month; batches TRACE history for at most five returned tranches.

name: trace_notional
description: Get estimated TRACE market-wide aggregate volume/notional over a rolling window. measure='volume' (default) is face/par dollars matching FINRA/BondTicker volume; measure='notional' is the price-adjusted cash proxy par × price/100. Entitled routes use the vendor capped-size estimate; public routes use FINRA's prior-month IG/HY and standard/144A category averages. Individual print rows remain 5MM+/1MM+ minimums. When: market volume, sector breakdown, sector x credit-grade matrix, net customer buying/selling by sector, most-active issuer names, or today-vs-yesterday comparison. NOT for: bond-level trade history (use prints_search or prints_group_by_period).

name: trace_volume_by_size
description: TRACE notional share of prints above a size threshold (block volume %). When: "% of TRACE volume for size > $5MM", "block trade share", "what percent of volume was blocks yesterday". Deterministic: ``block_notional / total_notional × 100`` for one trading day. Scope (priority): 1. ``cusips`` — explicit CUSIP list (use CUSIPs from a prior issuer list when the user is following up on an issuer drill-down). 2. ``issuer`` — fuzzy issuer match (same as securities_search). 3. neither — market-wide TRACE for that day. NOT for: market volume time series (use trace_notional), listing individual block prints (use prints_search with min_quantity), or volume surge rankings (use volume_surge_search).

name: movers_search
description: Biggest Movers: top bonds by magnitude of session-over-session change. When: "biggest movers", "wideners", "tighteners", "today vs yesterday", "price gappers" (approximate), or the movers for a specific past session ("movers on Thursday"). Pairs a session's last print per CUSIP with the most recent prior-session last print and returns the price and yield deltas. Date convention: "movers for day X" means X's last prints vs. the prior trading session (e.g. Thursday's movers = Thursday vs. Wednesday). To ask for a specific past day, resolve it to a date and pass ``as_of``; omit ``as_of`` for the latest session vs. its prior session. MULTI-SESSION ("5-day movers", "biggest N-day spread moves"): pass ``window_sessions=N`` (1-5). This compares the latest active session to the one N sessions earlier (nearest-prior-print endpoints) and, for N>1, returns a true ``g_spread_delta_bps`` (yield delta net of the Treasury-curve move at both endpoint dates) that the rows are re-ranked by. A slow-and-steady bond that never enters any single-day top-N cannot be reconstructed by chaining daily calls — use ``window_sessions`` for N-day/N-session move asks. OAS is out of scope. NOT for: intra-session rankings (use securities_search with order_by='notional'|'trades'), volume surges (not yet supported), or net customer buy/sell flow by sector (use trace_notional group_by='net_flow').

name: sector_activity_search
description: Sector Activity: one row per GICS sector with today's volume, trailing-20-day average notional, volume multiplier, and an average-yield delta as a spread-change proxy. When: "sector activity heatmap", "which sectors are most active today", "sector spread moves", "sector flow". Aggregates across every CUSIP that printed today grouped by ``GOLD_DIM.DIM_SECURITY.SECTOR``. NOT for: per-CUSIP rankings (use securities_search), single-bond session deltas (use movers_search), volume surges at the bond level (use volume_surge_search).

name: mktx_cpp_movers
description: Top movers ranked by CP+ EOD mid-price change over 1-90 calendar days. When: "top movers by CP+ EOD", "biggest CP+ price changes", CP+ gainers/losers — the CP+ (MarketAxess fair value) analogue of TRACE print movers. Each row carries BOTH CP+ mid values and their dates (the diff inputs), liquidity score, rating, sector, and 7-day TRACE trade count + notional. NOT for: TRACE print movers (use securities_search order_by price change) or CP+ history series (use mktx_history). Params: credit_grade 'I' (IG) or 'H' (HY), optional; direction 'up' | 'down' | 'abs' (default — biggest absolute moves); limit 1-50 (default 10); lookback_days 1-90 (default 1, use 30 for "last month"). Returns: {movers: [...], meta}. Surface returned dates, STALE_DAYS/GAP_DAYS, and PRICE_SUSPECT inline. Movers with LIQUIDITY_SCORE null have no current MKTX liquidity coverage — say so, don't guess.

lookback_days.description: Calendar-day lookback from 1 to 90; use 30 for last month.

name: mktx_cpplus
description: Get CP+ pricing, liquidity scores, and tradability matrix for bonds. When: CP+ prices, fair value, liquidity assessment, or tradability analysis. NOT for: historical CP+ trends (use mktx_history). Params: cusips (1-50 CUSIPs; 12-char ISINs are accepted and resolved to CUSIPs). Returns: CUSIP -> {issuer, ticker, coupon, maturity, cpp_mid, cpp_bid, cpp_offer, cpp_yld, cpp_ts, cpp_stale_days, cpp_is_stale, liq_score, tradability}. cpp_is_stale is session-aware (EOD feed: Friday's mark counts fresh through Monday); if true, present CP+ as a prior-close mark dated cpp_ts. ALWAYS use the returned issuer/ticker/coupon/maturity to identify the bond — do NOT infer the security name from the CUSIP or prior context. See system prompt for CP+ field semantics.

name: mktx_history
description: Get daily CP+ history with TRACE last-print comparison. When: CP+ price/yield trends over time (daily), or comparing CP+ EOD vs the day's last TRACE print. MKTX is EOD-only — one snapshot per CUSIP per day, no intraday. NOT for: current snapshot (use mktx_cpplus). Params: cusips (1-20 CUSIPs or ISINs), lookback_days (default 30, max 90). Returns: per CUSIP array of [{ts, mid, yld, bid, offer, last_px, diff_cents}, ...]. diff_cents is cash price diff in cents (last_px - mid) * 100; positive = trading rich, negative = cheap. meta.securities maps each CUSIP to its {issuer, ticker, sector, coupon, maturity} — use this for the bond identity/label; do NOT infer the security name from the CUSIP. Provenance: each row blends two sources — mid/bid/offer/yld come from MarketAxess CP+, while last_px and diff_cents come from FINRA TRACE prints. meta.source and the per-field source_fields block disclose this explicitly.

name: bond_lookalikes
description: Find similar bonds (look-alikes) for given CUSIPs. When: "similar bonds", "alternatives", "comparable", or relative value analysis. Params: cusips (1-5), num_recommendations per bond (default 5, max 20), include_same_issuer (default false), target_sector (optional GICS sector to swap INTO — e.g. "Pharmaceuticals, Biotechnology & Life Sciences" or a sector name; overrides the same-sector anchor so you can replace a Utility with Pharma comps of similar maturity/credit/spread). Returns: ranked matches with coupon_d, mat_d, sprd_d (spread bps: +WIDER/CHEAPER, -TIGHTER/RICHER), px_vs_cpp (cash price cents: +RICH/above CP+, -CHEAP/below CP+), similarity score, rationale.

name: issuer_bond_snapshot
description: Outstanding issuer bond table with multi-day G-spread change. When: "list all outstanding <issuer> bonds", issuer bond list with maturity / coupon / last price / yield / N-day spread change, ranked by most recently traded. Prefer this over ``securities_search`` whenever the user asks for a multi-day spread change column (``securities_search`` only has the current G-spread level; ``movers_search`` is 1-session yield delta market-wide, not per-issuer). NOT for: market-wide movers (use movers_search), single-CUSIP drill (use securities_search / prints_latest), or volume-by-size share (use trace_volume_by_size).

name: volume_surge_search
description: Volume Surge: bonds trading louder today than their trailing 20-day average. When: "volume surge", "volume spike", "unusual volume", "louder than usual", "today vs 20-day average". Pairs today's cumulative per-CUSIP notional with the per-active-day average over the prior 20 calendar days and returns the multiplier. NOT for: session-over-session price/yield moves (use movers_search), intra-session ranking (use securities_search with order_by='notional'). Params: - credit_grade: "IG" | "HY" | None (both). - sector: optional GICS sector label (e.g. "Industrials"). - min_multiplier: minimum ``today / avg`` ratio to surface (default 1.5 — filters bonds trading roughly at their normal pace). - min_trades_today: minimum prints today for a CUSIP to be eligible (default 1 — increase to 3+ to filter one-print noise). - limit: top N by multiplier (1-200, default 50). - offset: pagination offset for browsing past the top N (default 0). Returns: JSON ``{"bonds": [...], "meta": {...}}`` with the standard §4 envelope. Each row carries cusip, issuer, sector, credit_grade, volume_today, volume_avg_20d, volume_multiplier, trades_today, last_trade.

name: etf_reference
description: ETF constituent reference — membership, rating/sector mix, and holdings list. When: "what's in LQD", "LQD sector breakdown", "top holdings of HYG", "is <CUSIP> in LQD", ETF membership / constituent / rating-distribution asks. COVERAGE: only the two iShares ETFs LQD (investment grade) and HYG (high yield) have constituent holdings. Index families with factsheet profiles ONLY (Bloomberg US Corp, ICE BofA, USHY, ...) have NO per-bond membership — this tool cannot list their constituents; say so and offer LQD/HYG as the nearest held proxy. There is no movers/deltas mode (no prior-date holdings). Params: - ticker: ETF ticker (LQD or HYG). - detail: "summary" (default) — total count + weighted live yield/duration + sector and credit-grade distribution; or "constituents" — the paginated holdings list (cusip, name, sector, rating, weight). - limit / offset: pagination for detail="constituents" (default 25, max 100). Disclose truncation from meta.truncated / meta.total. Returns: JSON. summary -> {ticker, as_of_date, total_constituents, live_yield, target_duration, sector_weights_pct, credit_grade_distribution}. sector_weights_pct and credit_grade_distribution[*].weight_pct are both PERCENTAGES (0-100), each summing to ~100 — never fractions. constituents -> {ticker, as_of_date, constituents: [...], meta: {...}}. Absence of a CUSIP is NOT evidence it is un-held elsewhere — it means not in THIS ETF on this date. Never fabricate constituents, weights, or ratings.

name: get_rates_snapshot
description: Get benchmark rates and yields for market context. When: yield curve, index yields, policy rate queries, or macro backdrop. NOT for: bond spread calculations — securities_search includes spreads automatically.

name: bond_calculate
description: Bond calculator for price-yield, accrued interest, duration, DV01, and convexity. When: User asks to calculate bond yield, price, duration, DV01, accrued interest, convexity, or yield-to-worst for a specific bond. NOT for: Portfolio-level analytics — use portfolio_analytics instead. Inputs come from market data, not guesses: read coupon, maturity, and the CLEAN price (securities_search last_px is clean) before calling; never fabricate them. full_analytics / price_to_yield / ytw require a price. There is no yield→duration mode — to get duration from a yield, call yield_to_price first, then full_analytics with that price. Params: - calculation: One of 'price_to_yield', 'yield_to_price', 'full_analytics', 'accrued_interest', 'ytw' - coupon_rate: Annual coupon rate as percentage (e.g. 5.0 for 5%) - maturity_date: Bond maturity date (YYYY-MM-DD, absolute — not "7-year") - price: Clean price per 100 face, NOT dirty (required for price_to_yield, full_analytics, ytw) - yield_pct: Yield as percentage (required for yield_to_price) - settlement_date: Settlement date (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today; pass the trade date when pricing off a historical print) - day_count: Day count convention: '30/360', 'ACT/ACT', 'ACT/360' - face_value: Face value (default 100) - frequency: Coupon frequency per year (default 2 = semi-annual) - call_schedule: JSON array of call dates for YTW, e.g. '[{"date":"2028-01-15","price":102}]' - notional: Notional amount in dollars for scaled DV01 calculation

name: render_chart
description: Render an interactive chart in the user's chat interface. When: user asks for a visualisation, graph, chart, or plot of data. NOT for: dual-scale comparisons (e.g. price ~100 vs yield ~5%) — call twice with separate charts. Params: data (JSON string of [{...}, ...]), chart_type (bar|line|area|scatter|pie), title, x_key, y_keys, orientation (vertical|horizontal), stacked, show_legend, right_y_keys (secondary Y-axis for dual-scale charts, line/bar/area only), x_label/y_label (axis titles), x_min/x_max and y_min/y_max (explicit numeric domains; omit either bound for automatic scaling). For an issuer yield curve, set source_mode='issuer_yield_curve', issuer to the requested issuer, and data='[]'; the tool retrieves issuer bonds internally. Returns: confirmation JSON with chart metadata.

source_mode.description: Set to issuer_yield_curve to retrieve and project issuer bonds inside the tool.

issuer.description: Issuer name used when source_mode is issuer_yield_curve.

name: draft_trade_ticket
description: Draft a trade ticket and render it in the chat. When: user wants a ticket drafted, asks to copy/export/send a trade from the current insight, or has finished refining the action and is ready to act. Call this AFTER confirming the trade details with the user — do not draft tickets from unverified prices or sizes. Required: action (buy|sell|switch), primary_cusip (9-char CUSIP), primary_security ("MSFT 6.40 12/30"), portfolio (name, not id), notional (dollars as integer), rationale (1-2 sentences on the relative-value or portfolio reason). Optional: price (execution/agreed clean price per $100 face — when given the tool computes proceeds = notional * price/100; when omitted the ticket is flagged price_unverified so the desk fills the level), limit_price, min_clip, urgency (low|medium|high), time_in_force, execution_notes (list of bullet-style strings), to_cusip + to_security (only for switch, for the target leg), impact_yield / impact_duration / impact_sector (estimated portfolio effect, e.g. "+3-5bps", "Neutral", "+0.6% Technology"). Basket sells: pass legs=[{side, cusip, security, notional, price?}, ...] to draft ONE reconcilable ticket for a multi-bond order (proceeds computed and summed across legs) instead of N separate single-CUSIP tickets. The primary fields still describe the headline leg. Returns: validated ticket JSON. Tool-computed proceeds and the price_unverified flag (per leg) are echoed in the returned payload; the chat UI renders the ticket as a copyable text block, CSV/XLSX/PDF downloads, and a Send-to-Desk button.

name: search_tools
description: Find additional (long-tail) tools by keyword and get their calling schema. When: the request needs a capability none of your first-class tools cover — issuer snapshot/overview, volume by trade-size bucket, volume-surge detection, platform release notes, issuer fundamentals, issuer equity context, ETF constituents / rating & sector distribution (LQD, HYG). Params: query — a few keywords (e.g. "volume by size", "fundamentals leverage"). Returns: matching tools as {name, summary, parameters} — then invoke with call_tool(name, arguments). Never guess a tail tool's name or parameters: search first.

name: call_tool
description: Invoke a long-tail tool found via search_tools. When: after search_tools returned the tool's schema. Params: name (exact tool name from the search result), arguments (JSON object string matching that schema; omit or "{}" when the tool takes no arguments). Returns: the tool's own JSON output, verbatim.

name: get_insight
description: Fetch a single insight by its UUID, scoped to the current user. When: the user opens a chat thread from an insight card, or references an insight by id. The chat's opening prompt is intentionally terse — just the insight and portfolio UUIDs — so call this FIRST to load the full title, summary, suggested_action, and supporting_data before responding. Do not ask the user for these details; they're in the graph.

name: news_search
description: Search recent financial news articles. When: news, headlines, market events, or articles about a topic. Coverage: only the ~100 most recent Reuters/Greenwich RSS items (50 per source) — a zero-hit here is NOT evidence a story does not exist; verify via headline (web) search before concluding. Params: query (search term, optional), source ('reuters'|'greenwich'|omit for all), limit (1-50, default 10), offset (default 0). Returns: JSON ``{"articles": [...], "meta": {...}}`` per §4.

name: remember_user_fact
description: MANDATORY: Call this IMMEDIATELY when user shares personal information. TRIGGERS (call this tool when you see these patterns): - "My favorite X is Y" → remember_user_fact(fact="favorite X is Y") - "I like X" → remember_user_fact(fact="likes X") - "I work at X" → remember_user_fact(fact="works at X") - "I prefer X" → remember_user_fact(fact="prefers X") Parameters: - fact: The fact to store (e.g., "favorite company is Apple") - category: Optional category like "preference", "company", "interest"

name: recall_user_facts
description: MANDATORY: Call this BEFORE answering questions about user preferences. TRIGGERS (call this tool when you see these patterns): - "What is my favorite X?" → recall_user_facts(query="favorite") - "Do you remember X?" → recall_user_facts(query="X") - "What did I tell you?" → recall_user_facts() - Any question about user's preferences, favorites, or personal details NEVER say "I don't know" without calling this first. Parameters: - query: Search term to filter facts (e.g., "favorite", "company") - category: Optional category filter

name: get_release_notes
description: Fetch AIQ Insight release notes from GitHub. When: "what's new", "latest changes", "what changed in vX.Y.Z", or changelog queries.

version.description: specific tag e.g. "v1.2.3", empty for latest.

offset.description: pagination offset (default 0). Ignored when ``version`` is set.

name: fmp_fundamentals
description: Get issuer fundamental financial data from Financial Modeling Prep. Issuer-level equity fundamentals (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow, ratios, key metrics, profile). Fundamentals questions are IN SCOPE — use this tool both for credit context on bond issuers (leverage, coverage, cash flow vs debt load) and for direct fundamentals questions about an issuer (earnings, revenue, margins). Data is keyed by stock ticker (e.g. "LUMN", "AAPL"), not CUSIP — resolve the bond's issuer ticker first.

dataset.description: One of "income" (income statement), "balance" (balance sheet), "cashflow" (cash-flow statement), "ratios" (financial ratios), "key_metrics" (per-share/valuation metrics), or "profile" (company profile: sector, CIK, market cap, description).

limit.description: Number of periods to return, newest first (default 4, max 20).

offset.description: Periods to skip from the newest (0 = most recent).

name: eodhd_market_data
description: Get issuer equity market data (prices, quote, dividends) from EODHD. Cross-asset context for bond issuers: how the issuer's *equity* is trading alongside its bonds. Symbols are equity tickers in EODHD's ``TICKER.EXCHANGE`` form; bare US tickers are normalised ("LUMN" -> "LUMN.US"). For ``bond``, pass a CUSIP or ISIN instead.

symbol.description: Equity ticker (e.g. "LUMN" or "LUMN.US"); CUSIP/ISIN for the "bond" dataset.

dataset.description: One of "eod" (daily OHLCV history, newest first), "quote" (delayed live quote, single record), "dividends", "splits", "fundamentals" (company fundamentals), or "bond" (bond terms by CUSIP/ISIN). "fundamentals" and "bond" require a paid EODHD plan.

from_date.description: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) lower bound for eod/dividends/splits. Defaults to ~6 months back for "eod".

offset.description: Records to skip from the newest (0 = most recent).

name: generate_portfolio_proposal
description: Generate a deterministic portfolio proposal based on a benchmark. Use this tool when the user wants to build, create, or generate a new portfolio using structured criteria rather than manual bond selection. The optimizer uses CVXPY convex optimization to: 1. Fetch liquid bonds matching the benchmark's credit grade and maturity range 2. Filter by sector exclusions 3. Optimize bond selection based on the chosen objective 4. Enforce concentration limits (max % per issuer and sector)

benchmark_id.description: Benchmark to track. Options: - "IG_SHORT": 1-3 year Investment Grade (duration ~2y) - "IG_INTERMEDIATE": 3-5 year Investment Grade (duration ~4y) - "IG_BROAD": Broad Investment Grade (duration ~7y) - "HY_BROAD": High Yield (duration ~4y)

target_yield_pickup_bps.description: Target yield relative to the selected benchmark in basis points (25 means benchmark +0.25%). Mutually exclusive with target_yield and targets_json.target_yield.

sector_exclusions.description: List of GICS sectors to exclude. Valid GICS sectors: Communication Services, Consumer Discretionary, Consumer Staples, Energy, Financials, Health Care, Industrials, Information Technology, Materials, Real Estate, Utilities

objective.description: Optimization goal. Options: - "max_yield": Maximize portfolio yield within duration constraint (income-focused) - "min_tracking_error": Minimize deviation from benchmark duration and sector weights (best for index-tracking portfolios) - "max_liquidity": Maximize trading volume (best for portfolios requiring execution) - "balanced": Weighted combination of yield (60%) and liquidity (40%)

weighting_strategy.description: Position sizing approach. Options: - "optimized": Use CVXPY optimization weights (default - maximizes objective) - "equal": Equal weight all selected bonds

min_position_size.description: Minimum position value in USD (default $100K). Positions below this threshold are dropped and remaining positions are rescaled.

min_bond_age_days.description: Exclude bonds issued within the last N days (default 14). New issues lack stable pricing and trade history. Set to 0 to include all.

use_marketaxess.description: Enable MarketAxess data enrichment (CP+ pricing, liquidity scores, tradability). Only works if user has an active MKTX licence.

targets_json.description: Optional JSON object string with target overrides for deterministic checks/output. Supported keys: target_duration, target_yield, max_issuer_pct, max_sector_pct, num_holdings.

name: get_portfolio_drift
description: Analyse drift between a portfolio's current state and its original construction targets. Use when: User asks about portfolio drift, deviation from benchmark, or how a portfolio has changed since it was built. Only works for portfolios built via the construction wizard (i.e., those with a saved benchmark_id and construction parameters). Returns target vs actual for duration, yield, and sector weights.

name: get_available_benchmarks
description: Get list of available portfolio benchmarks with their characteristics. Use this tool to show the user what benchmarks are available for portfolio construction. Call this before generate_portfolio_proposal if the user hasn't specified a benchmark.

name: portfolio_create
description: Create a new user-managed portfolio or watchlist. Use when: User asks to "create", "make", or "set up" a new portfolio or watchlist. Args: ctx: Tool context (auto-supplied) containing HumanUserProfile. name: Portfolio name (e.g., "High Yield Strategy", "My Watchlist") description: Optional description type: Portfolio type. Options: - "LIVE": Real holdings (requires quantity/price) - "MODEL": Simulated/Strategy holdings (requires quantity/price) - "WATCHLIST": Monitoring only (quantity/price optional/ignored) Returns: JSON with new portfolio details.

name: portfolio_add_holding
description: Add security holdings to a portfolio (batch operation). Use when: User asks to "add", "buy", or "put bonds into my portfolio".

holdings.description: List of holdings to add (max 100). Each holding has: - cusip: CUSIP identifier (9 chars, required) - quantity: Notional amount in dollars (e.g., 1000000 = $1mm notional) - purchase_price: Clean price per $100 face (e.g., 98.5 = $98.50 per $100 face, NOT decimal 0.985) - purchase_date: Purchase date (ISO format YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to now)

name: portfolio_remove_holding
description: Remove security holdings from a portfolio (batch operation). Use when: User asks to "remove", "sell", "delete", or "take bonds out of my portfolio". Args: portfolio_id: Portfolio ID cusips: List of CUSIP identifiers to remove (max 25, each 9 chars) Example: ["912828ZT6", "037833AK6"] Returns: JSON with updated portfolio holdings and per-item results.

name: portfolio_swap
description: Swap one bond for another in a portfolio (atomic remove/reduce + add). Use when: User asks to "swap", "replace", "switch", or "trade out" a bond for another.

sell_cusip.description: CUSIP of bond to sell (9 chars)

buy_cusip.description: CUSIP of bond to add (9 chars)

buy_quantity.description: Notional amount in dollars for the new bond (e.g., 1000000 = $1mm)

buy_price.description: Clean price per $100 face for the new bond (e.g., 98.5 = $98.50 per $100)

sell_quantity.description: Optional notional to sell. If None, sells entire position. If provided, reduces position by that amount.

name: portfolio_list
description: List all user-managed portfolios. Use when: User asks to "show", "list", "get", or "see my portfolios" (user's holdings). Args: name: Optional case-insensitive portfolio-name search. Use this when the user names a portfolio so older matches are not hidden by paging. Returns: JSON with up to 10 newest matching portfolios and pagination metadata.

name: portfolio_list_holdings
description: List all holdings in a portfolio with current market data and P&L. Use when: User asks to "show", "list", "get", or "view holdings in" a specific portfolio. Args: portfolio_id: Portfolio ID Returns: JSON with holdings array and portfolio analytics.

name: portfolio_analytics
description: Get detailed analytics for a portfolio including allocation, metrics, and P&L. Supports two modes: - **Persisted**: Pass `portfolio_id` to analyze an existing portfolio from the database. - **Draft**: Pass `holdings` array to analyze a portfolio before saving (for preview/validation). Use when: - User asks for "analytics", "performance", "allocation", or "metrics" for a portfolio. - You need to validate a draft portfolio before creating it.

holdings.description: List of holdings for draft analysis. Each item requires: - cusip: 9-character CUSIP - quantity: Notional amount in dollars (e.g., 1000000 = $1mm) - purchase_price: Clean price per $100 face (e.g., 98.5 = $98.50 per $100) - purchase_date: Optional ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD)

name: portfolio_liquidation_analysis
description: Estimate days to liquidate each position based on TRACE ADV and a participation rate cap. When: User asks about liquidation time, exit time, how long to sell/unwind a portfolio. Formula: daily_tradable = ADV × participation_rate; estimated_days = position_notional ÷ daily_tradable. Assumptions (returned in the ``assumptions`` block, do not present as exact): par/face-value basis (≡ price=100); ADV = average daily PAR traded over a 30-calendar-day TRACE window; portfolio weighted_avg_days/max_days EXCLUDE no-volume names, so the portfolio figure skews optimistic (AIQ-2021/2022).

participation_rate.description: Fraction of ADV to trade per day (default 0.25 = 25%). Range 0.05–0.50.

name: portfolio_delete
description: Delete a portfolio and all its holdings. Use when: User asks to "delete", "remove", or "get rid of" a portfolio. Args: ctx: Tool context (auto-supplied) containing HumanUserProfile. portfolio_id: Portfolio ID to delete Returns: JSON confirmation.
